channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump White House rescinds order freezing federal grants after widespread confusion
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's budget office on Wednesday rescinded an order freezing spending on federal grants, less than two days after it sparked widespread confusion and legal challenges across the country, according to two people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Sundance Film Festival goes online this week. Here’s how to watch the films
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — Access to the Sundance Film Festival doesn’t require a trip to Park City, Utah, anymore — just an internet connection. Over half of the films that premiered this past week will be available to steam on the festival’s online platform starting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida Republican introduces bill to add Trump to Mount Rushmore
item.description: The Interior Department would be in charge of undertaking Trump’s addition to the South Dakota landmark if approved by Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Defense secretary pulls Trump critic Gen. Milley's security clearance and protective detail
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is pulling security protections and clearance for Gen.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Flawed emergency alert systems lagged when residents needed them most during Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When disaster strikes, government emergency alert systems offer a simple promise: Residents will get information about nearby dangers and instructions to help them stay safe.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, CLAUDIA LAUER, AMY TAXIN and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Things to know about the Trump administration order on car and pickup fuel economy
item.description: Hours after being sworn in as the new U.S. Secretary of Transportation, Sean Duffy took aim at the main way the federal government regulates miles per gallon for cars and pickup trucks — also a principal way that it controls air pollution and addresses climate change.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump appeals hush money criminal conviction
item.description: President Trump appealed his New York hush money criminal conviction and has brought on a new team of attorneys to represent him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump approval rating slips after flurry of executive actions: Survey
item.description: President Trump's approval rating slipped after his first week back in the Oval Office, where he issued a slew of executive orders.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Capitol Police arrest man with Molotov cocktails attempting to target Johnson, Hegseth, Bessent
item.description: The U.S. Capitol Police arrested a man with Molotov cocktails and knives who was allegedly attempting to kill House Speaker Mike Johnson and President Trump’s two recently confirmed nominees, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard involved in board meeting brawl between boyfriend, activist
item.description: Chicago-area "super mayor" Tiffany Henyard jumped into a Thornton Township board meeting fist fight between her boyfriend and an activist who criticized her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s DOJ could shift key Supreme Court cases
item.description: President Trump’s Justice Department could change the department’s position on critical Supreme Court cases, potentially altering the trajectory of high-profile appeals before the justices.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: I tried out the RV you can move around with an iPad
item.description: RVing has been around for a long time, but one California startup is looking to reinvent things. They're building the Pebble Flow, an all-electric, high-tech travel trailer that does a lot of the tricky stuff all by itself - from parking to hitching and even leveling out at your campsite.
item.dc:creator: KTLA 5

item.title: Live updates: White House rescinds funding freeze, RFK Jr. faces tough questions
item.description: President Trump's executive actions, including a freeze on federal grants and a buyout offer to federal employees, have drawn criticism from Democrats and some Republicans, while his pick for HHS Secretary, Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Young people 'inspired' by Trump and Musk, UCLA survey finds
item.description: (KTLA) -- A large number of young people say they are "inspired" by the likes of Elon Musk and Donald Trump and want to be like them, a survey from UCLA found.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: States lose access to Medicaid payment portal amid Trump federal aid freeze
item.description: State Medicaid programs across the country are reporting they've lost access to federal payment portals one day after President Trump announced a freeze on federal grants and aid.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: Mother and musician fights ovarian cancer with community help
item.description: Yawei was initially diagnosed in the United States with a Hernia, after multiple delays it became apparent it was not just a Hernia but stage four ovarian cancer.
item.dc:creator: Esmeralda Zamora, NewsNation

item.title: These are the most romantic restaurants in Las Vegas, according to OpenTable
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Valentine's Day is just around the corner and finding the perfect restaurant to take the one you love can always be challenging.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Weather forces Blue Origin to scrub launch of short space trip
item.description: The payloads were to experience at least two minutes of lunar gravity forces, a first for New Shepard.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, Fox 5

item.title: American Airlines worker struck, killed by vehicle at Charlotte Airport
item.description: A worker at Charlotte Douglas International Airport (CLT) died Monday after being struck by an airline ramp vehicle on the tarmac, local police have confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, WGN 9

item.title: Border agents shot at in South Texas
item.description: Border Patrol agents were fired upon Monday in the remote Starr County town of Fronton, Texas, Border Report has been told.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Apple software update will activate AI by default, but there is a way to turn it off
item.description: iPhone users could have a somewhat surprising new feature on their devices after their next software update.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Family, friends of Gabby Petito share details surrounding her death in upcoming Netflix documentary
item.description: An upcoming Netflix documentary will shed new light on the story of Gabby Petito and her death at the hands of her fiancé, Brian Laundrie.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: First US case of H5N9 bird flu strain found in California
item.description: The first confirmed case of H5N9 bird flu in the United States has been identified in Merced County, California, and control measures are being undertaken to contain the virus.
item.dc:creator: Dom McAndrew, WGN 9

item.title: Family, friends of Gabby Petito share details surrounding her death in upcoming Netflix documentary
item.description: An upcoming Netflix documentary will shed new light on the story of Gabby Petito and her death at the hands of her fiancé, Brian Laundrie.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, ABC 4

item.title: Full-scale replica of Anne Frank's hidden annex opens in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A full-scale replica of the secret annex where Anne Frank penned her famous diary opened in New York City on Monday as the world marked International Holocaust Remembrance Day.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO and JOSEPH FREDERICK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman gets more than 3-year prison term for helping pregnant Chinese women get to US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California woman was sentenced Monday to more than 3 years in prison in a long-running case over a business that helped pregnant Chinese women travel to the United States to deliver babies who automatically became American citizens. U.S. District Judge R.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecutors say woman charged in killing of Border Patrol agent was in contact with homicide suspect
item.description: A Washington state woman charged in connection with the fatal shooting of a U.S. border patrol agent in Vermont had been in frequent contact with someone whom authorities have linked to homicides in Pennsylvania and California, a federal prosecutor said in court documents Monday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3.8 magnitude earthquake in Maine felt in Massachusetts, Rhode Island
item.description: A Ware, Massachusetts, resident said she was home and her whole house was shaking for about 10 seconds from what she at first believed was an 18-wheeler coming down Route 9.
item.dc:creator: Emma McCorkindale, Fox 5

item.title: Pardoned Jan. 6 rioter killed by Indiana deputy
item.description: JASPER COUNTY, Ind. — An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Beneficiaries under Social Security Fairness Act could be waiting 'more than one year' for adjusted payments, SSA says
item.description: The Social Security Administration, in an update to its website on Friday, said the department does not currently have the funding to execute the adjustments “in a timely manner and without negatively affecting day-to-day customer service.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender people from military service
item.description: A fact sheet for Monday’s executive order refers to transgender Americans as “trans-identifying,” a technical term used by some to delegitimize transgender identities.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, ABC 4

item.title: Is cell service getting worse? One study suggests that it is
item.description: The study, from J.D. Power, showed an increase in reported wireless network problems between the first and the second half of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Penske's pair of Porsches dominate as The Captain wins Rolex 24 at Daytona for 2nd consecutive year
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Roger Penske started his 59th season in motorsports the same way he closed last year — as a champion.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mel Gibson’s ‘Flight Risk’ is No. 1 at box office, ‘The Brutalist’ expands
item.description: Critics lambasted it and audiences didn’t grade it much better. But despite the turbulence, Mel Gibson’s “Flight Risk” managed to open No. 1 at the box office with a modest $12 million, according to studio estimates Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, Fox 5
